baseline_solo.reasoning_summary: CMRX exhibits severe fundamental distress (negative earnings, negative margins, minimal revenue) in an overheat macro regime requiring strict quality discipline. While analyst sentiment has improved (1 SB, 4 B as of Mar 2025 vs. prior holds), the company's Q3 2024 financials show -$22.9M net loss on $26k revenue, indicating pre-revenue or near-zero-revenue biotech status. The 60-day price surge (+476%) and Wikipedia attention spike (17-34 views/day Feb-Mar) suggest retail/speculative interest, but alternative data corroboration is absent: industry-specific adapters not wired, ownership data unavailable, information integrity assessment flags low-tier-only sources with no primary corroboration. The single news item (dopamine antagonist market commentary) is descriptive only and does not constitute a catalyst. Macro regime (overheat, 20% equity cap) and information integrity veto (uncorroborated, low-tier sources) combine to block both surge_short and quality_long sleeves. Insufficient evidence and missing alt-data verification mandate fail-closed decision.

Assess analyst rating improvement (1 SB, 4 B vs. prior 0 SB, 0 B) for recency and conviction; check if driven by new data or sentiment reversion
Obtain forward guidance, management commentary, or investor presentation to contextualize revenue trajectory and cash burn rate
Cross-check dopamine antagonist market commentary against company's actual pipeline stage and competitive position
Evaluate short interest, borrow availability, and float to assess surge_short sleeve eligibility if fundamental thesis improves
